mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS changes are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.5% indicates a tight labor market that triggers wage-cost pressures and margin compression for non-labor-intensive industries, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.5%, or the mean return in triggered periods does not significantly underperform the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A negative interaction between the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation—where inflation rises while the nominal policy rate stays depressed—compresses real interest rates and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 0.13% with CPI at 237.0 in mid-2015
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fail to produce a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals that supply-side capacity is rolling over ahead of demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified late-cycle periods, negative month-over-month INDPRO changes coincide with positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the Sharpe ratio of the conditional signal is below 0.2 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one million units per month reflects a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1000, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return in these periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls reveal a self-reinforcing demand-income loop that accelerates nominal GDP expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to achieve a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive co-movement does not predict positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, each month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the cumulative return of the signal is positive over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production is a coincident indicator of real economic output expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, or the strategy Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.
